original_text: A new superpower emerges on the world stage.
identified_property: benevolence and governance quality of the superpower

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: A ruthlessly authoritarian and aggressively expansionist new superpower, with no regard for human rights or international norms, emerges on the world stage.

variations.label: low
variations.text: A corrupt and repressive new superpower, prone to exploiting weaker nations and suppressing domestic dissent, emerges on the world stage.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: A self-interested new superpower with mediocre governance, limited transparency, and a tendency to prioritize its own advantage over broader cooperation, emerges on the world stage.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: A new superpower emerges on the world stage.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: A reasonably well-governed and diplomatically cooperative new superpower, committed to respecting international norms, emerges on the world stage.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A highly benevolent new superpower with strong democratic institutions, robust rule of law, and a genuine commitment to global welfare emerges on the world stage.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: An extraordinarily benevolent and transparently governed new superpower, dedicated to advancing human rights, equitable development, and lasting international peace, emerges on the world stage.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier varies the benevolence and governance quality of the emerging superpower along a single dimension. Tier 1 describes the most malevolent and poorly governed version; each successive tier improves governance quality and benevolence until Tier 7, which describes maximal benevolence and exemplary governance. The core scenario—a new superpower emerging—remains constant throughout.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because a superpower that is extraordinarily benevolent, transparently governed, and dedicated to human rights, equity, and peace would produce the best outcomes for both its own citizens and the world at large.
